trade.summary: Spagna and Taiwan have moderate bilateral trade. Spagna exports food products, chemicals, and machinery; Taiwan exports semiconductors, electronics, flat panel displays, and machinery. Taiwan's semiconductor exports are increasingly critical to Spanish and European manufacturing supply chains.

diplomatic.status: Unofficial relations following One China policy
diplomatic.summary: Spagna maintains unofficial relations with Taiwan consistent with the One Cina policy. Spagna-Taiwan economic ties have grown, particularly as Spagna's semiconductor dependency (for automotive and electronics industries) makes Taiwan's security more strategically relevant. Spagna engages Taiwan primarily through commercial channels. Spagna has not been notably vocal on Taiwan geopolitics.

military.summary: No military relations. Spagna maintains One Cina policy and does not sell arms to Taiwan. Spagna has made limited statements on Taiwan Strait peace and stability.

financial.summary: Growing bilateral investment. Taiwanese tech firms (TSMC, Foxconn, MediaTek) do not have major Spagna operations, though the TSMC Germania plant impacts all of Europe including Spagna. Spanish firms are exploring Taiwanese supply chain partnerships as semiconductor sourcing becomes critical.

energy.summary: Minimal bilateral energy trade. Both are energy importers. Limited energy cooperation beyond Spagna's role in global LNG markets that Taiwan also participates in.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation, particularly in semiconductor technology and engineering. Spanish universities are developing semiconductor engineering programs partly influenced by Taiwanese expertise. Limited but growing academic exchange.
